articles.title: Check E20 fuel-related issues with authorised auto dealers: ARAI
articles.description: ARAI says drivers suspecting E20 ethanol-blended petrol issues should visit authorised dealers; tests found 2–6% mileage drop, no engine problems.

articles.title: E20 gives 4% lower mileage but safe for engines: Experts
articles.description: E20 petrol (20% ethanol) is safe for engines but may cut mileage 3–4%, experts say; motorists advised to keep tanks 80% full to avoid moisture.

articles.title: E20 petrol triggers engine damage complaints at city fuel stations
articles.description: Mangaluru fuel stations report more engine damage complaints linked to E20 petrol as rainwater contamination and faulty fuel caps affect older vehicles.

articles.title: Industry says E20 petrol is safe for all vehicles, backs ethanol push for energy security
articles.description: Former Engineers India Ltd Chairman and Managing Director Vartika Shukla said E20 fuel was introduced in phases before being rolled out nationwide after extensive testing on two- and four-wheelers ahead of the 2030 mandate.

articles.title: Maruti Suzuki, Toyota India execs defend E20 petrol: ‘No problem in terms of wear & tear, corrosion'
articles.description: The cause of concern is what happens if E20 fuel is used in cars that have been manufactured and sold before 2023, which were primarily designed for E10? Read on to know what auto industry experts said.
